All 9 acceptance criteria met. 87 tests passing.
description:
Implement save/load of named layouts and automatic default layout persistence. A layout captures all window positions, sizes, working directories, and viewport state.

## What to Build

### Data Model

```typescript
interface Layout {
  name: string;
  sessions: Array<{
    title: string;
    cwd: string;
    position: { x: number; y: number };
    size: { width: number; height: number; cols: number; rows: number };
  }>;
  viewport: { panX: number; panY: number; zoom: number };
  gridSize: number;
}
```

### src/main/config/ConfigStore.ts

Uses electron-store for JSON persistence to OS-standard config directory:
- macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/smoke/
- Windows: %APPDATA%/smoke/

```typescript
interface SmokeConfig {
  defaultLayout: Layout | null;
  namedLayouts: Record<string, Layout>;
  preferences: { ... };  // from config issue
}
```

### IPC Handlers (add to src/main/ipc/ipcHandlers.ts)

- layout:save — handle({ name, layout }): if name === '__default__', save to defaultLayout; else save to namedLayouts[name]
- layout:load — handle({ name }): return defaultLayout or namedLayouts[name]
- layout:list — handle(): return Object.keys(namedLayouts)
- layout:delete — handle({ name }): delete from namedLayouts

### Preload API Additions

```typescript
layout: {
  save: (name, layout) => ipcRenderer.invoke('layout:save', { name, layout }),
  load: (name) => ipcRenderer.invoke('layout:load', { name }),
  list: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('layout:list'),
  delete: (name) => ipcRenderer.invoke('layout:delete', { name }),
}
```

### Auto-Save Default Layout

In renderer, subscribe to sessionStore changes with 2-second debounce:
- Serialize current layout (all session positions/sizes/CWDs + viewport state)
- Save as '__default__' via smokeAPI.layout.save
- 2s debounce prevents hammering disk during drag operations

### Restore on Launch

In App.tsx initial mount:
- Load '__default__' layout
- If exists: for each session in layout, spawn new PTY with saved CWD, position window at saved coordinates
- Viewport restored to saved pan/zoom
- Note: shell history NOT preserved (PTYs are ephemeral). Only positions, sizes, CWDs restored.

### src/renderer/layout/LayoutPanel.tsx

UI for managing named layouts:
- Save current layout with a name (text input + save button)
- List saved layouts
- Load a layout (closes existing sessions, creates new ones)
- Delete a layout
- Accessible from sidebar footer or toolbar

### src/renderer/layout/useLayoutPersistence.ts

Hook encapsulating the auto-save logic and restore-on-mount.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Current layout auto-saves every 2 seconds (debounced) to __default__
- On app launch, default layout restored (sessions created in correct positions with correct CWDs)
- User can save current layout with a name
- User can load a named layout (existing sessions closed, new ones created at saved positions)
- User can delete a named layout
- User can list all saved layouts
- Layout includes: session positions, sizes, CWDs, titles, viewport pan/zoom, grid size
- Saved config is human-readable JSON in OS config directory
- Loading a layout with a CWD that no longer exists gracefully falls back to default CWD
notes: COMPLETED: ConfigStore (electron-store), IPC handlers (save/load/list/delete), preload API, useLayoutAutoSave (2s debounce), useLayoutRestore (restore on mount + load named), LayoutPanel UI (sidebar footer), App.tsx integration, CWD fallback in PtyProcess. DECISIONS: auto-save subscribes to both sessionStore and canvasStore; LayoutPanel lives in sidebar footer with toggle. 87 tests passing.
title: Layout Persistence: Save/Load/Default Layouts

close_reason: Fixed: buildOnMainThread() yield frequency changed from every 50 to every 10 files to reduce event loop stalls. Added fastByteLength() in TerminalOutputBuffer to skip Buffer allocation for ASCII strings (common case), reducing GC pressure under sustained output. Regression tests added.
description:
SearchIndex.ts:198-214: buildOnMainThread() fallback iterates all files with setImmediate() yields only every 50 files. For 10K+ file projects, main thread stalls for multiple seconds. TerminalOutputBuffer.ts:38-80 also creates GC pressure under sustained high output — stripAnsi() + Buffer allocations on every data chunk.

Fix: Increase yield frequency in main-thread fallback (every 10 files). Consider moving to worker thread unconditionally. Pool or reuse Buffer allocations in TerminalOutputBuffer.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: buildOnMainThread() yielded only every 50 files causing multi-second stalls; TerminalOutputBuffer called Buffer.byteLength on every append creating GC pressure. FIX: Changed yield frequency to every 10 files; added fastByteLength() that fast-paths ASCII strings (the common case) without Buffer allocation. REGRESSION TEST: yes — 3 tests for fastByteLength correctness.
title: Search index main-thread fallback blocks event loop for large projects

close_reason: Completed: electron-builder config for Mac (dmg/zip, x64+arm64) and Windows (NSIS, x64), macOS entitlements plist, notarization script, asarUnpack for node-pty, app icons, and packaging npm scripts
description:
Configure electron-builder to produce distributable packages for macOS (.dmg) and Windows (NSIS installer). Includes native module bundling, code signing, and platform-specific handling.

## What to Build

### electron-builder config in package.json

```json
{
  "build": {
    "appId": "com.smoke.app",
    "productName": "Smoke",
    "directories": { "output": "dist" },
    "files": ["out/**/*", "node_modules/node-pty/**/*"],
    "asarUnpack": ["node_modules/node-pty/**/*"],
    "mac": {
      "category": "public.app-category.developer-tools",
      "target": [
        { "target": "dmg", "arch": ["x64", "arm64"] },
        { "target": "zip", "arch": ["x64", "arm64"] }
      ],
      "hardenedRuntime": true,
      "gatekeeperAssess": false,
      "entitlements": "build/entitlements.mac.plist",
      "entitlementsInherit": "build/entitlements.mac.plist"
    },
    "win": {
      "target": [{ "target": "nsis", "arch": ["x64"] }]
    },
    "nsis": {
      "oneClick": false,
      "perMachine": false,
      "allowToChangeInstallationDirectory": true
    },
    "afterSign": "scripts/notarize.js"
  }
}
```

### Critical: asarUnpack for node-pty

node-pty includes native .node binaries and a spawn-helper executable. These CANNOT be loaded from inside an ASAR archive. asarUnpack extracts them to app.asar.unpacked/ at install time.

### macOS Entitlements (build/entitlements.mac.plist)

Required because node-pty uses JIT compilation and unsigned memory:
- com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit: true
- com.apple.security.cs.allow-unsigned-executable-memory: true
- com.apple.security.cs.allow-dyld-environment-variables: true

### Notarization Script (scripts/notarize.js)

Uses @electron/notarize:
- Only runs on darwin
- Reads APPLE_ID, APPLE_ID_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID from env
- Notarizes the .app bundle after signing

close_reason: Completed: xterm.js terminal rendering with WebGL addon, FitAddon, cached character dimensions for CSS-transform compatibility, PTY IPC bridge (onData/onExit filtering by sessionId), TerminalWidget mount point, terminal.css styles, Canvas.tsx updated to render real terminals. 8 unit tests for calculateTerminalSize. All acceptance criteria met.
description:
Mount xterm.js terminals inside canvas-positioned divs and connect them to the PTY backend via IPC. Handles xterm.js lifecycle, WebGL renderer setup, and the critical CSS-transform compatibility workaround.

## Critical Known Issue

xterm.js CharSizeService uses getBoundingClientRect() which returns dimensions AFTER CSS transforms. When the canvas is scaled (zoomed), xterm.js computes wrong character sizes, causing layout bugs.

WORKAROUND: Initialize xterm.js terminals at scale=1. When canvas zoom changes, do NOT rely on FitAddon auto-sizing. Set terminal dimensions (cols/rows) explicitly based on the LOGICAL (pre-transform) size of the container. Measure character dimensions once at creation, cache them.

```typescript
function calculateTerminalSize(widthPx: number, heightPx: number, charWidth: number, charHeight: number) {
  return {
    cols: Math.max(2, Math.floor(widthPx / charWidth)),
    rows: Math.max(1, Math.floor(heightPx / charHeight))
  };
}
```

## What to Build

### src/renderer/terminal/useTerminal.ts

xterm.js lifecycle hook:
- Creates Terminal instance with: cursorBlink: true, fontFamily: 'Menlo, Monaco, "Courier New", monospace', fontSize: 13, lineHeight: 1.2, scrollback: 10000, allowTransparency: false (faster), theme: { background: '#1a1a2e', foreground: '#e0e0e0' }
- Loads FitAddon (for initial sizing only)
- Opens terminal into container ref
- Loads WebglAddon AFTER open (requires canvas context). Handle onContextLoss by disposing WebGL addon (xterm falls back to canvas renderer automatically).
- Stores terminalRef, fitAddonRef, webglAddonRef as useRef
- Cleanup: terminal.dispose() on unmount
- Expose getSnapshot() method: captures last N lines from terminal buffer for thumbnail mode

### src/renderer/terminal/usePty.ts

IPC bridge hook connecting xterm.js to PTY backend:
- Subscribe to window.smokeAPI.pty.onData — filter by sessionId, call terminal.write(data)
- Subscribe to terminal.onData — call window.smokeAPI.pty.write(sessionId, data)
- Subscribe to window.smokeAPI.pty.onExit — display exit message in terminal, update session status
- Return cleanup functions for all subscriptions

Performance note: onData callback receives events for ALL sessions, each terminal filters by event.id === sessionId. This is O(N) per event. If N>20 terminals becomes a bottleneck, refactor to centralized dispatcher with Map<id, callback> for O(1) lookup. Start simple.

### src/renderer/terminal/TerminalWidget.tsx

Mount point component:
- Renders a div ref as container
- Calls useTerminal(containerRef, sessionId, cols, rows)
- Calls usePty(sessionId, terminalRef)
- CSS class "terminal-container" with width/height 100%

### src/renderer/styles/terminal.css

```css
.terminal-container {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.terminal-container .xterm {
  height: 100%;
  padding: 4px;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- xterm.js terminal renders inside a canvas-positioned div
- Typing sends keystrokes to PTY and output appears
- WebGL renderer active (verify: single canvas element, no xterm-text-layer)
- Full scrollback works (scroll up to see history)
- Terminal renders correctly at different canvas zoom levels (no character size distortion)
- onContextLoss gracefully falls back to canvas renderer
- Process exit displayed in terminal with exit code
- 10000 lines of scrollback preserved
notes: COMPLETED: useTerminal (xterm.js lifecycle, WebGL addon, FitAddon, cached char dims, getSnapshot), usePty (IPC bridge with sessionId filtering, exit handling), TerminalWidget (mount point), terminal.css, Canvas.tsx updated to use real terminals. Tests: 8 unit tests for calculateTerminalSize. DECISIONS: Inlined calculateTerminalSize test logic to avoid importing browser-dependent xterm modules in vitest.
title: Terminal Rendering: xterm.js + WebGL on Canvas

description:
Implement the sidebar panel showing all terminal sessions with bidirectional highlight sync and pan-to-session. ## What to Build ### src/renderer/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx Left panel (240px default width): - Header: "Sessions" title + "+ New" button - Session list: sorted by createdAt, maps to SessionListItem components - Reads sessions from sessionStore - Reads focusedId for active highlight ### src/renderer/sidebar/SessionListItem.tsx Individual session entry: - Shows: status dot (green=running, gray=exited), session title, shortened CWD path - onMouseEnter: sessionStore.highlightSession(session.id) — highlights corresponding canvas window - onMouseLeave: sessionStore.highlightSession(null) - onClick: pan canvas to center on this session (animated), focus session - CSS classes: focused (bold/active state), exited (dimmed), highlighted ### src/renderer/sidebar/useSidebarSync.ts Pan-to-session hook: - Takes sessionId, reads session position/size from store - Calculates target pan to center session in viewport: targetPanX = viewportWidth/2 - (session.x + session.width/2) * zoom targetPanY = viewportHeight/2 - (session.y + session.height/2) * zoom - Animates pan using requestAnimationFrame with ease-out over 300ms - Also calls sessionStore.focusSession(id) ### Bidirectional Highlight Sync Canvas -> Sidebar: - When terminal window focused on canvas (clicked), sessionStore.focusSession(id) updates focusedId - SessionListItem reads focusedId and applies "focused" CSS class Sidebar -> Canvas: - When SessionListItem hovered, sessionStore.highlightSession(id) updates highlightedId - TerminalWindow reads highlightedId and applies "highlighted" CSS class (visible glow/border) ### src/renderer/App.tsx Layout ```tsx function App() { return (
); } ``` CSS: app-layout uses display: flex. Sidebar: width: 240px, flex-shrink: 0. Canvas: flex: 1. ### src/renderer/styles/sidebar.css - Dark background matching app theme - Session items with hover states - Status dots: green (#4caf50) for running, gray (#666) for exited - Focused item: brighter background, left border accent - Highlighted item: subtle background change - CWD text: truncated with ellipsis, smaller font, muted color ## Acceptance Criteria - Sidebar shows all active sessions with title, CWD, and status dot - Hovering sidebar item highlights corresponding canvas window (visible border/glow) - Hovering canvas window highlights corresponding sidebar item - Clicking sidebar item smoothly pans canvas to center that window (300ms ease-out animation) - Clicking sidebar item focuses the window (brings to front) - "+ New" button creates a new terminal session - Session list updates in real-time on create/close - Exited sessions visually distinct (dimmed, different dot color) - Sidebar does not re-render when canvas pan/zoom changes (perf)

description:
Comprehensive audit of all performance-critical and stability-critical code paths in the application. This goes beyond the initial code review (smoke-zg6b, smoke-cftb, smoke-w1dz, smoke-1dt8, smoke-kvrb) to cover the full surface area.

Audit targets:

**Renderer hot paths:**
- Canvas render loop: measure frame times during pan/zoom with 20+ elements. Profile React re-renders — identify unnecessary renders from unstable selectors, missing memoization, or state churn.
- Viewport culling: verify O(n) scan of sessions is fast enough at scale. Benchmark with 50, 100, 200 elements.
- xterm.js lifecycle: audit terminal create/destroy/reattach for memory leaks (detached DOM nodes, orphaned event listeners, leaked WebGL contexts).
- Zustand store subscriptions: ensure no store update triggers a cascade of re-renders across unrelated components.
- CodeMirror instances: check for leaked editor instances when file viewers are closed or culled.

**Main process hot paths:**
- PTY data throughput: profile the data path from node-pty → output buffer → IPC → renderer under sustained high output (e.g. find / or cat large-file).
- IPC message volume: measure IPC message rate during active terminal sessions. Identify if batching or throttling is needed.
- Search index build: profile CPU and memory during initial indexing of large projects (10K+ files). Ensure it doesn't starve the main thread.
- File watcher event storms: test behavior when hundreds of files change simultaneously (git checkout, npm install). Verify debouncing prevents UI stall.

**Memory stability:**
- Long-session memory profile: run the app for 1+ hour with active use, monitor memory growth. Identify leaks from terminals opened/closed, files viewed, layouts saved.
- AI conversation history: verify conversations are bounded and don't grow unbounded in memory.
- Plugin lifecycle: ensure plugin load/unload doesn't leak.

**Error resilience:**
- Audit all IPC handle() handlers for unhandled exceptions that crash the main process.
- Verify error boundaries exist around every major renderer component tree.
- Test graceful degradation when external dependencies fail (PTY spawn failure, filesystem permission denied, network timeout).

Output: a prioritized list of findings as new beads issues, each with specific file/line references and reproduction steps.
notes:
COMPLETED: Full codebase audit across renderer, main process, memory stability, and error resilience. Created 12 prioritized findings as new beads issues.

ISSUES CREATED:
P0 (3):
- smoke-24lq: IPC handlers missing try/catch — crashes main process
- smoke-curu: Unbounded hidden buffer in terminalRegistry
- smoke-d6u8: Zustand selector cascade re-renders

P1 (6):
- smoke-rvc9: No error boundaries around core renderer components
- smoke-fir5: PTY data IPC has no batching or backpressure
- smoke-c6hs: Unbounded AI conversation/agent maps
- smoke-8wgf: File watcher event storms
- smoke-e51m: CodeMirror instances leak on viewport culling
- smoke-46hk: Unhandled promise rejections in renderer

P2 (3):
- smoke-57c8: Viewport culling O(n) scan optimization
- smoke-0mxb: Search index main-thread fallback blocking
- smoke-unbk: Silent error swallowing in file ops

DECISIONS: Grouped related findings into coherent issues rather than filing one per line. P0 items are app-crashing or severe memory/perf issues. P1 items cause degraded experience. P2 items are optimization opportunities.
title: Deep codebase audit: hot performance paths and stability hardening

On reattach, flushHiddenBuffer writes the buffered data into the terminal before usePty takes over, preventing blank renders after viewport re-entry with active processes.
description: When a terminal with an active process (e.g. Claude Code running) goes off-screen via panning and then comes back into the viewport, the terminal renders as blank despite the process still running. This is a follow-up to smoke-1gp which addressed the initial reattach issue but did not fully resolve it for active/streaming processes.
title: Terminal still renders blank after returning to viewport with active process

close_reason: Fixed: Debounce timers reset endlessly during event storms (npm install, git checkout), allowing pendingUpdates queues to grow unbounded and flooding renderer with IPC. Added BURST_LIMIT=50 to SearchIndex and FilenameIndex — queues flush immediately when limit reached. Rate-limited sendProgress (250ms) and notifyUpdated (500ms) IPC calls. FileWatcher.ts timer cleanup was already correct. Regression tests added.
description:
SearchIndex.ts:443-484 and FilenameIndex.ts:154-204 both use fs.watch() with 500ms debounce, but have no burst protection for event storms (git checkout, npm install, large builds).

Issues:
- SearchIndex pendingUpdates can grow very large between debounce intervals — no count limit
- FilenameIndex has same pattern — no burst protection
- FileWatcher.ts:31 can accumulate debounce timers if watch/unwatch is called rapidly — timer not cleared on unwatch
- sendProgress() IPC notifications are not rate-limited, flooding renderer during bulk changes

Impact: npm install or git checkout triggers hundreds of file change events, causing IPC flood and UI stall.

Fix: Add burst count limits to pending update queues, rate-limit IPC progress notifications, clean up debounce timers on unwatch.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: File watcher debounce timers reset endlessly during event storms (npm install, git checkout), allowing pendingUpdates queues to grow unbounded and sendProgress IPC to flood the renderer. FIX: Added BURST_LIMIT=50 to both SearchIndex and FilenameIndex — flushes immediately when queue exceeds limit. Rate-limited sendProgress (250ms) and notifyUpdated (500ms) IPC calls. FileWatcher.ts:31 already handles timer cleanup correctly — no change needed. REGRESSION TEST: yes (SearchIndex + FilenameIndex).
title: File watcher event storms — insufficient debouncing for bulk changes

close_reason: Completed: Infinite canvas with GPU-accelerated pan/zoom via CSS transform3d. useCanvasControls hook with ref-based pan/zoom (no React re-renders), wheel-to-pan, ctrl+wheel zoom-to-cursor, middle-mouse/space+drag pan with pointer capture, SVG repeating grid pattern with fade-out below 0.3 zoom, Canvas component with session placeholders, canvas.css with will-change and transform-origin 0 0. 12 tests covering zoom math, clamping, grid opacity, and pan logic.
description:
Implement the infinite canvas with GPU-accelerated pan and zoom. This is the spatial container for all terminal windows. Performance is paramount — the canvas MUST feel as responsive as native scroll, with zero perceptible lag. ## Architecture: Three Nested DOM Layers ```html
style="transform: translate3d(panX, panY, 0) scale(zoom)"> {sessions.map(s => )}
``` ## What to Build ### src/renderer/canvas/useCanvasControls.ts (MOST PERFORMANCE-CRITICAL) Manages pan/zoom entirely through refs and direct DOM manipulation, bypassing React reconciliation: - panRef, zoomRef, isPanningRef, lastPointerRef — all useRef, NO useState - applyTransform() — directly sets viewportRef.current.style.transform = translate3d(x,y,0) scale(z) - WHEEL handler: scroll to pan (deltaX/deltaY), ctrl/meta+scroll to zoom toward cursor - MIDDLE MOUSE / SPACE+DRAG: pan with pointer capture - Zoom-to-cursor math: newPanX = cursorX - (cursorX - oldPanX) * (newZoom / oldZoom) - Zoom clamp: 0.1 to 3.0 - On pan-end/zoom-end: sync values to Zustand canvasStore (debounced) for persistence - All event listeners added with { passive: false } for wheel to allow preventDefault ### src/renderer/canvas/Grid.tsx SVG repeating pattern for grid lines: - Uses element for efficiency (not individual lines) - Grid scales naturally with viewport transform (it's a child of viewport div) - Fade out when zoom < 0.3: opacity = Math.min(1, (zoom - 0.3) * 2) - Return null when zoom < 0.3 (not just invisible — remove from DOM) - Pattern: M gridSize 0 L 0 0 0 gridSize with rgba(255,255,255,0.06) stroke ### src/renderer/canvas/Canvas.tsx Outer component: - Contains canvas-root div and viewport ref - Calls useCanvasControls(viewportRef) - Maps sessions to TerminalWindow components (placeholder divs for now) - Gets sessions from sessionStore ### src/renderer/styles/canvas.css ```css .canvas-root { position: relative; overflow: hidden; width: 100%; height: 100%; cursor: grab; } .canvas-root.panning { cursor: grabbing; } .canvas-viewport { position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; transform-origin: 0 0; /* CRITICAL: zoom from top-left */ will-change: transform; /* GPU layer promotion */ } ``` ## Critical Implementation Details - transform-origin MUST be 0 0 (not center) for the zoom-to-cursor math to work - will-change: transform promotes the viewport to its own compositing layer (GPU) - translate3d (not translate) forces GPU compositing even on browsers that wouldn't otherwise - Space key for pan: track keydown/keyup at document level, set spaceKeyDown flag. Only works when no terminal is focused. - Pointer capture on middle-mouse/space-drag prevents events from leaking to terminals ## Acceptance Criteria - Scroll wheel pans the canvas (deltaX for horizontal, deltaY for vertical) - Ctrl+scroll (or pinch on trackpad) zooms, centered on cursor position - Middle-mouse-drag pans the canvas - Space+drag pans the canvas (when no terminal focused) - Canvas transform uses translate3d + scale (verify in DevTools: element has own compositing layer) - Grid lines visible and scale with zoom - Grid fades out when zoom < 0.3 - Pan/zoom at 60fps (verify with DevTools Performance tab) - Zoom clamps between 0.1 and 3.0 - Pan/zoom values sync to canvasStore on interaction end
title: Infinite Canvas: Pan/Zoom/Viewport with CSS Transform3d

description:
Multiple Zustand store hooks return reference types (Set, object) without shallow equality checks, causing unnecessary re-renders across all consuming components on every state update.

Affected hooks:
- sessionStore.ts:426 useSelectedIds() — returns Set<string>, triggers re-render of ALL window components when any selection changes
- sessionStore.ts:427 (nearby) useHighlightedId() — same issue
- activityStore.ts:39-43 useActiveIds() — returns Set<string>, causes Minimap to redraw on every PTY data event
- preferencesStore.ts:60 usePreferences() — returns entire Preferences object, any pref change cascades globally

Impact: Selecting one window re-renders 50-200 windows. PTY data causes constant Minimap flicker. Theme/font changes cascade to all components.

Fix: Add useShallow wrapper or implement custom equality comparators for Set/object returns. Ensure components use granular selectors (usePreference(key) instead of usePreferences()).
notes: ROOT CAUSE: Zustand selectors for Set/object types (useSelectedIds, useActiveIds, usePreferences) used default Object.is equality, causing cascade re-renders on every unrelated state update. FIX: Wrapped all three selectors with useShallow from zustand/react/shallow which performs shallow Set/object comparison. useHighlightedId was already correct (returns primitive). REGRESSION TEST: yes — 7 tests in shallowEquality.test.ts.
title: Zustand store selectors cause cascade re-renders — missing shallow equality

description:
FileEditorWidget.tsx:34-92: When a FileViewerWindow with editing=true is viewport-culled, the component unmounts and CodeMirror cleanup runs. But when the window scrolls back into view, a new CodeMirror instance is created from scratch. This causes:
- Double memory allocation if old instance cleanup is delayed
- Potential orphaned DOM contexts
- User loses editor state (cursor position, undo history)

Reproduction: Open file in edit mode → pan canvas so file viewer is culled → pan back → file re-renders with new CodeMirror instance.

Fix: Either preserve CodeMirror state across culling (like terminals use terminalRegistry) or ensure clean destruction and state restoration.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: Canvas.tsx returned null for culled file viewers, fully unmounting FileEditorWidget and destroying CodeMirror. Terminals avoided this by staying mounted with CSS visibility:hidden. FIX: When editing=true, keep FileViewerWindow mounted but hidden via CSS (visibility:hidden, pointerEvents:none) instead of returning null. Non-editing file viewers still unmount normally. REGRESSION TEST: No — this is a rendering/lifecycle fix verified by existing test suite (120 tests pass).
title: CodeMirror instances leak on viewport culling in edit mode

description:
Set up the complete project skeleton using electron-vite as the build tool with React + TypeScript.

## What to Build

Initialize the Electron app with three-process architecture (main, preload, renderer) using electron-vite for fast HMR.

## Dependencies to Install

**dependencies:** react, react-dom, @xterm/xterm, @xterm/addon-webgl, @xterm/addon-fit, node-pty, electron-store, uuid, zustand
**devDependencies:** electron, electron-vite, @electron/rebuild, electron-builder, typescript, @types/react, @types/react-dom, @types/uuid, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react

## Key Files to Create

- package.json — with all deps, scripts (dev, build, start, rebuild), and postinstall for electron-rebuild
- electron.vite.config.ts — externalize node-pty (native C++ addon can't be bundled by Vite), configure react plugin for renderer
- tsconfig.json (base), tsconfig.node.json (main+preload), tsconfig.web.json (renderer with jsx: react-jsx)

- postinstall script must run electron-rebuild to compile node-pty against Electron's Node headers
- BrowserWindow sandbox must be false so preload can use Node APIs for the IPC bridge

- "build": "electron-vite build"

description:
Implement the full lifecycle of terminal sessions: creation (with CWD, optional auto-launch), focusing, closing (with PTY cleanup), and canvas interactions.

## What to Build

### Session Creation (3 entry points)

1. Sidebar "+ New" button — creates terminal at center of current viewport
2. Double-click empty canvas — creates terminal at click position (grid-snapped)
3. Cmd/Ctrl+N keyboard shortcut — creates terminal at center of current viewport

Creation flow:
1. Determine CWD: preferences.defaultCwd if set, else the directory Smoke was launched from (process.cwd() / app.getPath())
2. Generate UUID for session ID
3. Determine position: center of viewport OR click position, snap to grid
4. Spawn PTY via smokeAPI.pty.spawn({ id, cwd, shell, args })
5. Add session to sessionStore with default size (640x480, 80x24)
6. If autoLaunchClaude enabled, PTY manager sends claude command after shell init

### Canvas Double-Click Handler (in useCanvasControls.ts)

```typescript
const onDoubleClick = (e: MouseEvent) => {
  // Only on empty canvas, not on terminal windows
  if ((e.target as HTMLElement).closest('.terminal-window')) return;
  
  // Convert screen -> canvas coordinates
  const rect = canvasRoot.getBoundingClientRect();
  const canvasX = (e.clientX - rect.left - panRef.current.x) / zoomRef.current;
  const canvasY = (e.clientY - rect.top - panRef.current.y) / zoomRef.current;
  
  // Snap to grid
  const snappedX = Math.round(canvasX / gridSize) * gridSize;
  const snappedY = Math.round(canvasY / gridSize) * gridSize;
  
  createNewSession({ x: snappedX, y: snappedY });
};
```

### Focus Management

- Clicking anywhere on terminal window: sessionStore.focusSession(id) + bringToFront(id)
- ONLY the focused terminal's xterm.js instance calls terminal.focus(). All others call terminal.blur().
- This ensures keyboard input goes to exactly one terminal
- Clicking empty canvas unfocuses all terminals

### Closing a Session

1. User clicks close button OR Cmd/Ctrl+W
2. Kill PTY: smokeAPI.pty.kill(id)
3. Wait for onExit confirmation (timeout 5s, then force-remove)
4. Dispose xterm.js terminal (frees WebGL context)
5. Remove session from sessionStore

### Process Self-Exit

When PTY exits on its own (user types 'exit'):
- Display exit message: [Process exited with code N]
- Change session status to 'exited' in store
- Keep window open so user can see last output
- User must explicitly close the window

## Acceptance Criteria
- Double-clicking empty canvas creates terminal at that position (grid-snapped)
- "+ New" sidebar button creates terminal at viewport center
- Cmd/Ctrl+N creates terminal at viewport center
- New sessions open in configured CWD
- Auto-launch Claude runs claude command when enabled
- Clicking terminal focuses it and brings to front
- Only focused terminal receives keyboard input
- Close button kills PTY and removes window after cleanup
- typing 'exit' in shell changes status but keeps window open
- No zombie PTY processes after closing (verify with ps aux)
- Screen-to-canvas coordinate conversion correct at all zoom levels
title: Session Lifecycle: Create, Focus, Close, Double-Click-to-Create

description:
Implement global keyboard shortcuts for common operations and proper focus management so keyboard input goes to the right place.

## What to Build

### Shortcut Map (src/renderer/shortcuts/shortcutMap.ts)

| Shortcut | Action | Context |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Cmd/Ctrl+N | New session | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+W | Close focused session | When session focused |
| Cmd/Ctrl+Tab | Cycle to next session | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Tab | Cycle to previous session | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+1..9 | Focus session by index | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+0 | Reset zoom to 100% | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+= | Zoom in | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+- | Zoom out | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+S | Save layout | Global |
| Cmd/Ctrl+, | Open settings | Global |
| Space (held)+drag | Pan canvas | When no terminal focused |
| Escape | Unfocus terminal (return to canvas) | When terminal focused |

### Focus Management Challenge

When a terminal is focused, keystrokes go to xterm.js -> PTY. But global shortcuts (Cmd+N etc) must still work. Solution:

1. Use xterm.js attachCustomKeyEventHandler to intercept keys BEFORE xterm processes them:

```typescript
terminal.attachCustomKeyEventHandler((event) => {
  if (event.metaKey || event.ctrlKey) {
    const shortcut = resolveShortcut(event);
    if (shortcut) {
      executeShortcut(shortcut);
      return false;  // prevent xterm from handling
    }
  }
  if (event.key === 'Escape' && event.type === 'keydown') {
    blurTerminal();
    return false;
  }
  return true;  // let xterm handle normally
});
```

2. Document-level keydown listener with { capture: true } fires in capture phase, before xterm's bubble-phase handler.

### src/renderer/shortcuts/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts

Global shortcut handler:
- Detects platform (Mac vs Windows) for Cmd vs Ctrl
- Registered on document with { capture: true }
- Calls preventDefault() on matched shortcuts
- Routes to appropriate actions (createSession, closeSession, cycleSession, etc.)

### Cycle Session Logic

Cmd+Tab cycles through sessions sorted by createdAt:
1. Get current focusedId
2. Find its index in sorted session list
3. Move to next (or previous with Shift) session
4. Focus it + pan canvas to it (reuse sidebar pan-to logic)

### Platform Detection

```typescript
const isMac = navigator.platform.includes('Mac');
const modKey = isMac ? 'metaKey' : 'ctrlKey';
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cmd/Ctrl+N creates new session even when terminal is focused
- Cmd/Ctrl+W closes focused session
- Cmd/Ctrl+Tab cycles through sessions (focus + pan-to)
- Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Tab cycles backwards
- Cmd/Ctrl+1-9 jumps to session by index
- Escape unfocuses terminal (keyboard stops going to PTY)
- Clicking terminal re-focuses it
- Cmd/Ctrl+0 resets zoom to 1.0
- Cmd/Ctrl+=  and Cmd/Ctrl+- zoom in/out
- All shortcuts work on macOS (Cmd) and Windows (Ctrl)
- Terminal keys (arrows, Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D, etc.) still work when terminal focused
- Space+drag pans canvas only when no terminal focused
title: Keyboard Shortcuts and Focus Management

description:
Implement the PTY management layer in the Electron main process. This is the core backend that spawns shell processes, pipes data bidirectionally over IPC, handles resizing, and manages process lifecycle.

## What to Build

### src/main/pty/PtyProcess.ts
Wraps a single node-pty instance:
- Constructor takes: id (string), cwd (string), shell (optional, defaults to platform default), args (optional), env (optional), cols (default 80), rows (default 24)
- Determine shell: options.shell || process.env.SHELL (mac) || 'powershell.exe' (win)
- Spawn via ptySpawn(shell, args, { name: 'xterm-256color', cols, rows, cwd, env: { ...process.env, ...env } })
- Emit 'data' events on pty output, 'exit' events on process exit
- Methods: write(data), resize(cols, rows), kill()
- Track exited state to prevent writes to dead processes

### src/main/pty/PtyManager.ts
Manages all PTY instances:
- Map<string, PtyProcess> for storage
- Methods: spawn(options) -> PtyProcess, get(id), write(id, data), resize(id, cols, rows), kill(id), killAll()
- killAll() called on app quit to prevent zombie processes

### src/main/ipc/channels.ts
Channel constants and TypeScript message type definitions:
- PTY_SPAWN: 'pty:spawn' (invoke/handle - request/response)
- PTY_DATA_TO_PTY: 'pty:data:to-pty' (one-way, renderer -> main)
- PTY_DATA_FROM_PTY: 'pty:data:from-pty' (one-way, main -> renderer)
- PTY_RESIZE: 'pty:resize' (one-way)
- PTY_KILL: 'pty:kill' (one-way)
- PTY_EXIT: 'pty:exit' (one-way, main -> renderer)

### src/main/ipc/ipcHandlers.ts
- pty:spawn — use ipcMain.handle (request/response, renderer needs the result)
- pty:data:to-pty — use ipcMain.on (fire-and-forget for keystroke performance)
- pty:resize — use ipcMain.on
- pty:kill — use ipcMain.on
- PTY output and exit events sent via mainWindow.webContents.send

### src/preload/index.ts
Expose typed smokeAPI.pty object via contextBridge:
- spawn(options) -> ipcRenderer.invoke('pty:spawn', options)
- write(id, data) -> ipcRenderer.send('pty:data:to-pty', { id, data })
- resize(id, cols, rows) -> ipcRenderer.send('pty:resize', { id, cols, rows })
- kill(id) -> ipcRenderer.send('pty:kill', { id })
- onData(callback) -> subscribe to 'pty:data:from-pty', return unsubscribe function
- onExit(callback) -> subscribe to 'pty:exit', return unsubscribe function

### src/preload/types.ts
TypeScript type definitions for the smokeAPI that both preload and renderer can reference.

## Critical Performance Decision
Use ipcMain.on (NOT handle) for pty:data:to-pty because keystrokes must be fire-and-forget with zero await overhead. Use webContents.send for PTY output (not invoke response). Only pty:spawn uses handle/invoke.

## Cleanup
Register app.on('before-quit', () => ptyManager.killAll()) in main/index.ts.

## Platform Detection
getDefaultShell(): process.platform === 'win32' ? (process.env.COMSPEC || 'powershell.exe') : (process.env.SHELL || '/bin/bash')

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can spawn a PTY from renderer via window.smokeAPI.pty.spawn({ id, cwd })
- Keystrokes sent via write() appear in the PTY
- PTY output streams back to renderer via onData callback
- resize() changes terminal dimensions (verified with stty size)
- kill() terminates the process and triggers onExit
- All PTYs killed on app quit (no zombie processes)
- Works on macOS (zsh/bash) and Windows (powershell/cmd)
title: PTY Backend: Main Process PTY Management + IPC

description:
Implement window chrome (title bar, close button) around each terminal, along with drag-to-move and drag-to-resize with grid snapping.

## What to Build

### src/renderer/terminal/TerminalWindow.tsx

Wraps TerminalWidget with window chrome and interaction handlers:
- Positioned absolutely on canvas: left/top from session.position, width/height from session.size
- z-index from session.zIndex
- CSS classes for focused, highlighted (sidebar hover), dragging states
- onPointerDown calls bringToFront(session.id)
- Contains: WindowChrome (top), terminal-body (middle, contains TerminalWidget), ResizeHandles (edges)

### src/renderer/window/useWindowDrag.ts

Drag state machine:
- onPointerDown on title bar: stopPropagation (prevent canvas pan), capture pointer, record start position + mouse position
- onPointerMove: calculate delta, DIVIDE BY ZOOM (critical — 100px mouse move at 0.5x zoom = 200px canvas move). Update session position in real-time via sessionStore.updateSession.
- onPointerUp: snap to grid — Math.round(position / gridSize) * gridSize. Animate snap with CSS transition (150ms ease-out).
- The zoom value must be passed in as a parameter. Getting this wrong causes drift/acceleration bugs.

### src/renderer/window/useWindowResize.ts

Resize state machine:
- Similar to drag but modifies width/height
- Resize handles on: right (e), bottom (s), bottom-right (se)
- Mouse deltas divided by zoom (same as drag)
- On resize-end: snap dimensions to grid, recalculate terminal cols/rows using calculateTerminalSize(snappedWidth, snappedHeight - CHROME_HEIGHT, charWidth, charHeight), call window.smokeAPI.pty.resize(sessionId, cols, rows)
- Minimum size: 10 grid cells wide, 8 grid cells tall (with default 20px grid = 200x160px minimum)

### src/renderer/window/useSnapping.ts

Grid snap utilities:
- snap(value) -> Math.round(value / gridSize) * gridSize
- snapPosition(pos) -> { x: snap(pos.x), y: snap(pos.y) }
- snapSize(size) -> { width: max(minWidth, snap(width)), height: max(minHeight, snap(height)) }
- During drag: calculate nearest grid lines, render thin guide lines at snap positions (visual feedback)

### src/renderer/window/WindowChrome.tsx

Title bar component:
- Status dot (green=running, gray=exited) 
- Session title (editable on double-click)
- Close button (x)
- Drag handlers attached to the chrome div (title bar is the drag handle)
- Height: 32px (CHROME_HEIGHT constant)

### src/renderer/window/ResizeHandle.tsx

Edge/corner resize handle:
- Positions: 'e' (right edge), 's' (bottom edge), 'se' (bottom-right corner)
- Renders as invisible hit areas (8px wide) with appropriate cursor styles
- On hover: show subtle visual indicator

## Critical: Zoom Compensation

ALL mouse deltas during drag and resize MUST be divided by the current canvas zoom factor:
```
canvasDeltaX = mouseDeltaX / zoom
canvasDeltaY = mouseDeltaY / zoom
```
Without this, windows drift faster at high zoom and slower at low zoom.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Terminal windows have title bar with title, status dot, and close button
- Dragging title bar moves window on canvas
- Drag works correctly at ALL zoom levels (no drift or acceleration)
- Releasing drag snaps window to nearest grid position (with smooth 150ms animation)
- Resize handles on right, bottom, and bottom-right edges
- Resizing snaps to grid on release
- Resizing triggers PTY resize (verify with stty size inside terminal)
- Minimum window size enforced (can't resize smaller than 200x160 with default grid)
- Clicking a window brings it to front (z-index updates)
- Dragging a window does NOT pan the canvas (event propagation stopped)
- Double-clicking title edits session title

description:
Implement the user-facing configuration system covering auto-launch Claude Code, default shell selection, grid size, and other preferences.

## What to Build

### Config Schema (in ConfigStore.ts)

| Key | Type | Default | Description |
|-----|------|---------|-------------|
| preferences.defaultShell | string | '' (system default) | Shell to spawn (e.g. /bin/zsh, powershell.exe) |
| preferences.autoLaunchClaude | boolean | false | If true, new sessions auto-run claude command |
| preferences.claudeCommand | string | 'claude' | Command to run for Claude Code |
| preferences.gridSize | number | 20 | Grid cell size in pixels |
| preferences.sidebarPosition | 'left' or 'right' | 'left' | Sidebar placement |
| preferences.sidebarWidth | number | 240 | Sidebar width in pixels |
| preferences.theme | string | 'dark' | Color theme |
| preferences.defaultCwd | string | '' | Default CWD (empty = app's CWD) |

### Auto-Launch Claude Code

When autoLaunchClaude is true, the PTY spawns a normal shell, waits a tick for init, then sends the claude command:

```typescript
// In PtyManager.spawn():
if (config.autoLaunchClaude) {
  setTimeout(() => {
    proc.write(config.claudeCommand + '\n');
  }, 100);
}
```

This approach keeps the shell alive after Claude exits (user can restart or do other work). Preferred over spawning claude directly as the PTY process.

### IPC Handlers

- config:get — handle(): return configStore.get('preferences')
- config:set — handle({ key, value }): configStore.set('preferences.{key}', value)

### Preload API

```typescript
config: {
  get: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('config:get'),
  set: (key, value) => ipcRenderer.invoke('config:set', { key, value }),
}
```

### src/renderer/config/ConfigPanel.tsx

Settings panel accessible from sidebar footer or menu:
- Shell selector (text input or dropdown of detected shells)
- Auto-launch Claude toggle with command input
- Grid size slider (10-50px range)
- Sidebar position toggle (left/right)
- Default CWD path input
- All changes save immediately via smokeAPI.config.set
- Changes to grid size update the grid immediately
- Changes to sidebar position re-layout immediately

## Acceptance Criteria
- Settings panel accessible from sidebar
- Changing defaultShell affects newly created sessions (existing sessions unaffected)
- Toggling autoLaunchClaude causes new sessions to auto-run claude command
- Claude command is configurable (not hardcoded to 'claude')
- Grid size preference updates grid immediately on change
- All preferences persist across app restarts
- Config file is human-readable JSON, editable by hand for power users
- Invalid config values are handled gracefully (e.g., non-existent shell path)
title: Configuration System: Auto-Launch Claude, Shell, Grid Preferences

close_reason: Completed: Replaced single-string buffer with chunked ring buffer using Buffer.byteLength for byte-accurate UTF-8 size tracking. Added throughput benchmarks for plain text, ANSI-heavy, and multibyte PTY output.
description:
PERF/MEMORY: The terminal output buffer enforces size via string.length (UTF-16 code units) not actual bytes — actual memory can exceed the intended cap for multibyte output. The append path builds existing+clean on every write and slices on overflow, causing frequent allocations and GC pressure under high PTY throughput (e.g. build logs).

Fix: track storage in bytes (Buffer.byteLength) or store as Buffer. Use chunked ring-buffer semantics (array/deque of chunks) to avoid full-string copies on each append. Add throughput benchmarks for long-running PTY sessions.
title: Rework TerminalOutputBuffer into byte-accurate chunked ring buffer
